The evidence suggests a balanced but slightly challenging path for Ukraine. The play-off path requires them to win two consecutive matches against potentially strong opponents. Given the competitive nature of European football and the fact that only 4 out of 22 teams in the play-offs will qualify, the baseline probability of Ukraine qualifying is relatively low.

The status quo outcome, where Ukraine has not yet qualified, carries significant weight. The world of football, particularly at the international level, tends to change slowly, and the play-offs are inherently high-pressure, unpredictable events.
